description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by three prominent metal hoops. The wood has a dark brown finish with visible grain patterns, while the hoops are a dull gray, suggesting aged or weathered metal. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and appear to be made of the same wood as the sides, tightly fitted within the metal bands. The barrel is fully closed, with no visible openings, spigots, or handles, and the surface texture is matte with a slightly rough appearance. There are no visible labels, engravings, or decorative markings on the exterior. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage areas, or near taverns and markets.
description: A dark wooden barrel with three gray metal hoops, bulging sides, and a flat, closed top. The surface is matte and slightly rough, with no visible markings or handles.
description_view_0: Front view showing the bulging sides, three metal hoops, and the flat, closed wooden top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature of the barrel and the even spacing of the metal hoops.
description_view_2: Another side view with similar features, emphasizing the wood grain and the tight fit of the top.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the continuous wood staves and metal hoops, with no visible openings or handles.
